search_queries_used: site:hrsonline.org 2023 remote device clinic ICD data review documentation alerts follow-up PDF
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic device programming, shock diagnosis, medicine, or emergency instruction
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Device data require the actual report and qualified device-clinic review.
Programming, urgency, diagnosis, and treatment remain specialist decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with an ICD or related CIED receiving in-clinic or remote follow-up.
setting_applicability: Qualified cardiac device clinic and remote monitoring program; this template is not an emergency monitoring system.
UAE_applicability: International HRS/EHRA/APHRS/LAHRS evidence requires UAE device-clinic credentialing, privacy, cybersecurity, emergency, programming, referral, and facility adaptation.
recency_verification: Current official HRS-hosted 2023 consensus PDF was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: The 2023 multisociety remote-device-clinic consensus remains current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The multisociety section supports documenting ICD and remote-monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device function.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting device and monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, patient education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device status.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The workflow section supports documenting that device data were received, triaged, reviewed, signed off, communicated, and linked to a clinician-entered plan without interpreting the data automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting that a transmission was triaged and reviewed by qualified staff, signed off, communicated, and linked to an already decided follow-up action.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The alert section supports recording received alerts, qualified review, patient communication, and clinic-defined action while emphasizing that remote monitoring is not an emergency system; it does not assign urgency or diagnose an event automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording received alerts, priority assigned by a qualified clinic, review, communication, and emergency-system limitations without generating alert programming, urgency, diagnosis, or treatment.

unresolved_source_gaps: Device type and manufacturer, implant indication and date, leads, battery, sensing, pacing, therapies, arrhythmia episodes, alerts, connectivity, transmission date, technical quality, symptoms, formal interpretation, programming, diagnosis, treatment, communication, emergency plan, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
